business.headline: Why has Diet Coke become costlier in India?The ongoing conflict in the Middle East has disrupted the supply of aluminium cans, forcing Coca-Cola to change the way it sources packaging for Diet Coke.

EMIs now consume almost entire family income for 60% of borrowersA survey by Expert Panel found many distressed borrowers are using fresh loans or cards to repay existing EMIs. The findings show debt stress is being driven by emergencies, income shocks and harsh recovery practices.

Banks, insurers to launch CKYC 2.0 in August: Will it make banking easier?CKYC 2.0 is an upgraded version of India's central KYC system. Instead of submitting identity and address proof separately to every bank, insurer or financial institution, customers will be able to share their existing verified records through a central registry.

Sensex ends 332 points lower, Nifty below 23,800; Eternal down 2%The BSE Sensex fell 331.62 points, or 0.43%, to close at 76,059.77, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 102.15 points, or 0.43%, to settle at 23,767.45.

Your manager didn't back you this appraisal cycle? Survey shows 1 in 3 felt sameThe nationwide survey found that 22% of employees said they received no support from their manager during appraisals, while another 10% said they were mostly on their own. Together, that means 32% of employees navigated the appraisal process without meaningful backing from their reporting manager.
Why are HCLTech shares rising even as the stock market trades in the red today?At the time of writing, HCL Technologies shares were trading at Rs 1,268.60, up 1.92%.

Why is Russia importing petrol from India despite being an oil giant?Russia has started importing petrol by sea from India to tackle shortages caused by Ukrainian attacks on Russian oil refineries and energy facilities, mentioned a report.

8th Pay Commission to hold Delhi consultations on Aug 7 & 10. What's next?The 8th Central Pay Commission has scheduled fresh Delhi consultations on August 7 and 10 for eligible employee associations that have already submitted memorandums. The move shows the panel is still gathering stakeholder views before framing recommendations on pay, pensions and allowances.

Will the July 31 ITR filing deadline be extended? Rumours vs realityWith only a few days left to file income tax returns, speculation over a possible extension is once again gathering pace. But is there any truth to the rumours?

Flipkart to launch food delivery service soon, taking on Zomato and SwiggyFlipkart Group CEO Kalyan Krishnamurthy confirmed that the company will launch its food delivery service within the next few weeks.

Infosys, Tech Mahindra, TCS fall up to 3%: Why are IT stocks falling todayIT stocks fell in early trade as the Wall Street technology selloff spread to Dalal Street. Rising crude, higher US yields and geopolitical worries added to expectations of near-term volatility.

Sensex down over 500 points: 3 reasons why the stock market is falling todayBenchmark indices extended their losses in early trade, with the BSE Sensex plunging 902.48 points, or 1.18%, to 75,488.91, while the NSE Nifty50 dropped 256.15 points, or 1.07%, to 23,613.45 around 10:20 am, as rising crude oil prices and weak global cues continued to weigh heavily on investor sentiment.

Sensex, Nifty tank in early trade lower as Brent crude tops $100The BSE Sensex fell 585.21 points, or 0.77%, to 75,806.18, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 173.65 points, or 0.73%, to 23,695.95 around 9:30 am.

AI bill catches up with Wall Street: Tesla crashes 14%, Alphabet falls 7%Tesla and Alphabet shares fell sharply after earnings highlighted the rising cost of AI investment. The reaction signalled that Wall Street is demanding clearer returns, cash flow and profit visibility.

Adani denies reports of airline plans, says not evaluating entry into aviationAdani Enterprises has denied reports that it is evaluating an entry into the airline business. The clarification follows speculation over rule changes and the group's wider aviation ambitions.

Why India's biggest airlines are worried about Adani's aviation ambitionsAdani's possible airline entry has prompted opposition from IndiGo and Air India. Both carriers say airport owners should remain neutral because they control vital infrastructure.

Will US scrap 10% tariff on India? Key deadline ends todayIf no new announcement is made, imports from India and several other US trading partners will revert to the normal Most Favoured Nation (MFN) tariff structure that was in place before April 2, 2025.
Crude oil crosses $100 for first time in two months as US-Iran conflict intensifiesAt the time of writing, Brent Crude, the global oil benchmark, was trading at $100.14 a barrel, up 6.45%.

Infosys to roll out salary hikes from October, hire 20,000 freshersThe IT major said the salary hike cycle will be implemented in three parts. While the company did not share details of the percentage increase, it confirmed that the rollout will begin in October and continue into January for different employee groups.

Infosys posts 12% jump in Q1 profit, trims upper end of FY27 growth outlookThe IT major posted a consolidated net profit of Rs 7,769 crore for the quarter ended June 30, 2026, compared with Rs 6,921 crore in the same period last year. Revenue from operations rose 14% to Rs 48,211 crore, up from Rs 42,279 crore a year ago.

entertainment.headline: All eyes on Thor as Avengers: Doomsday introduces MCU's biggest threat ever The Avengers: Doomsday trailer places Thor at the centre of Marvel's clash with Doctor Doom. His history across the MCU makes him the clearest link between the Avengers, Fantastic Four and X-Men.

Trolling or praising?: Sobhita reacts as Naga Chaitanya admires her cooking Actor Naga Chaitanya shared a dinner photo featuring actor-wife Sobhita Dhulipala serving him a meal. Their fun social media banter gave a glimpse of their married life.

Aparna Sen backs youth-led CJP protest, slams violence against unarmed students Veteran actor-filmmaker Aparna Sen backed students at Jantar Mantar in Delhi and said their peaceful dissent moved her to tears. Her remarks framed the demonstration as a constitutional lesson and condemned allegations that unarmed students were beaten.

Gauahar Khan, Mithila Palkar team up for ZEE5 prison drama Ikroop Actors Gauahar Khan and Mithila Palkar will feature in the upcoming film Ikroop. The prison-set drama centres on resilience, sisterhood and revenge within a broken justice system.

Aamir's daughter Ira has no opinion on students' protest, she doesn't 'look at news' Actor Aamir Khan's daughter Ira Khan said she did not comment on the student protests because she avoids news and social media. She said looking away is a privilege and that she is still figuring out the most helpful role aligned with her values.

Ben and Casey Affleck's mother, Chris, dies at 83 after battling pancreatic cancer Chris Anne Affleck, mother of actors Ben and Casey Affleck, died on June 2 after pancreatic cancer. Her final wish to see her grandson Atticus graduate was fulfilled two days earlier.

Prakash Raj backs Sonam Wangchuk, questions delay in Dharmendra Pradhan's exit Actor Prakash Raj reacted to activist Sonam Wangchuk ending his 26-day fast, thanking him for his sacrifice. He also questioned why Union Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation was still being delayed.

Actor Narayani Shastri claims rampant MDMA, cocaine drug use in TV industry Actor Narayani Shastri has claimed cocaine and MDMA are common in television parties and on sets. Her remarks also pointed to delayed shoots and a wider normalisation of substance use in the entertainment industry.

Plas Johnson, saxophonist behind Pink Panther theme, dies at 94 Plas Johnson, the jazz saxophonist behind the Pink Panther theme, has died at 94. His career also highlighted the overlooked role of session musicians in popular music.

Amaal Mallik slams Tanishk Bagchi over song theft, misconduct with female singers Music composer Amaal Mallik has accused composer-singer Tanishk Bagchi of stealing songs, copying music and mistreating female singers in a lengthy note. The attack escalates the Saiyaara royalty row and widens it into a public dispute over credits, conduct and industry politics.

Lock Upp's Akanksha says she'd rather meet her dog than husband Gaurav Khanna After Vinny Arora met Dheeraj Dhoopar, Akanksha Chamola told Shreya Kalra and Shilpa Shinde that she wished a family member had visited her instead of Gaurav Khanna. The exchange mixed humour with honesty and again put focus on Akanksha's relationship with Gaurav.

Explained: The MGR scene in Jana Nayagan and why Vijay had to include it The AI-generated MGR sequence has emerged as Jana Nayagan's most talked-about moment. Here's why Vijay's farewell film brings back Tamil cinema's biggest icon, and what the emotional tribute adds to the story and his political journey.

Karavali review: Prajwal-Raj B Shetty's film offers thrills but feels incompleteRelease Date:Fri Jul 24, 2026 Karavali movie review: With beautiful visuals and intriguing ideas, Gurudatta Ganiga's coastal drama, starring Prajwal Devaraj with Raj B Shetty in an extended cameo, offers moments of genuine thrill but ultimately feels incomplete due to its lack of clarity and depth.

Jana Nayagan fan review: Thank you, Thalapathy Some farewells end with applause. Vijay's Jana Nayagan one ends with hope, uncertainty, and millions quietly thanking a man who gave them a lifetime of memories.

What to watch on OTT this week: Exciting new releases on Netflix, Prime and more Are you up for some amazing content on OTT? This week, digital platforms are bringing a binge-worthy mix of thrill, romance, comedy and drama, ensuring your watchlist stays packed and your popcorn bowl stays busy.

The India Story review: A relevant issue lost in an underwhelming courtroom dramaRelease Date:Fri Jul 24, 2026 The India Story movie review: The India Story follows a grieving father and a novice lawyer as they challenge toxic food practices in court. The film keeps the food safety debate in focus, but its drama rarely matches the issue's urgency.

Horse riding, knives, guns: Inside Vijay Deverakonda's brutal training for Ranabaali Vijay Deverakonda underwent months of horse-riding, weapons and combat training for his next period drama, Ranabaali. The preparation was designed to make his action sequences look authentic on screen. India Today Digital has exclusive details.

Only 6 Sunny Deol films got an A rating in last 26 years. Batwara 1947 is the 7th Sunny Deol's Batwara 1947 has received an A certificate from the censor board. The rating underlines the film's Partition setting and its un-softened portrayal of violence and loss. It is also a rare film in Deol's career spanning 43 years.

Spine over career: Actor Ayesha Khan to troll warning her against protest support Dhurandhar actor Ayesha Khan was recently detained in Mumbai while trying to join students protesting over NEET irregularities. Her reply to a warning about losing Bollywood work has drawn further attention to her public support for the movement.

general news.headline: 400 criminals found loitering near Jantar Mantar protest site in 5 days: PoliceDelhi Police's Facial Recognition System identified around 400 people with criminal records in and around the Jantar Mantar protest site between July 20 and July 24, with some suspects also linked to the July 20 vandalism, sources said.

Thank you, friends: PM expresses gratitude for response to his midnight videoPrime Minister Narendra Modi thanked youngsters for the overwhelming response to his late-night video, saying their love and suggestions had strengthened his bond with them.

Do I still need to prove my sincerity: Wangchuk slams those alleging 'deal' with govtEarlier in the day, his wife Gitanjali Angmo had appealed to critics not to judge him, revealing that Wangchuk is recovering in the ICU after losing 11 kg during the fast.

Union Minister of State for Railways Ravneet Singh Bittu resignsUnion Minister of State for Railways, Ravneet Singh Bittu has resigned from the Union Council of Ministers, with President Droupadi Murmu accepting his resignation with immediate effect, according to a press communique issued by Rashtrapati Bhavan on Friday.

Is Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation in zone of consideration?From a flat no to seeking a day's time, this is the first time that the issue of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation has come closest to the zone of consideration.

CWG Day 2 Live: Jhandu Kumar wins bronze in heavyweight para powerliftingCommonwealth Games 2026, Day 2 Live updates: India are on the board at the Commonwealth Games 2026. Jhandu Kumar clinched bronze in the men's 72kg para powerlifting event after Nigeria's Riluwan Idris failed to clear 212kg on his final attempt, confirming India's first medal of the Games.

28 Indian crew members safe after LPG tanker Disha attacked in Iranian watersMozambique-flagged LPG tanker Disha was attacked by an unknown missile in Iranian territorial waters near the Strait of Hormuz. All 28 Indian crew members aboard the vessel were reported safe, with the Indian Embassy in Tehran coordinating with authorities.

Misquoted: CJI on 'don't have time to watch videos' remark amid protest crackdownChief Justice of India Surya Kant said he was "misquoted" in media reports amid the CJP protest controversy, reiterating that the Supreme Court is open to all and never refuses to hear cases, while stressing that judicial procedures must be followed.

How murder by a drunken prince made Saudi Arabia go dry for 70 yearsThe death of Saudi prince Abdullah bin Fahad from a cocktail of alcohol and drugs in a London hotel has shocked the world. Over 70 years ago, a murder committed by a Saudi prince after a night of drunken revelry made Saudi Arabia adopt one of the toughest prohibition laws in the world.

Army officer's son, 20, dies as car falls into ditch in Delhi's Vasant KunjA 20-year-old son of an Indian Army officer, pursuing higher education in the US, died after the car he was driving skidded on a slippery road, fell into a drain and overturned in Delhi's Vasant Kunj.

Govt plans biggest NTA reset yet after NEET row, 47 officials removed: SourcesThe Centre is preparing a major overhaul of the National Testing Agency following the NEET-UG controversy. Sources say around 47 officials have been removed, outsourcing practices will change, and structural and technological reforms will be introduced to create a leak-proof examination system for national entrance tests.

Hot pizzas, gone in minutes: Delivery man looted near Jantar MantarA pizza delivery worker was looted by a crowd near Jantar Mantar's Tolstoy Chowk in New Delhi on Friday, with a video of the incident reaching Delhi Police.

2026 Maruti Suzuki Brezza launched at Rs 7.40 lakh, gets a new turbo-petrol engineMaruti Suzuki has launched the 2026 Brezza in India at Rs 7.40 lakh, introducing a turbo-petrol engine, six-speed manual gearbox, upgraded features, refreshed styling and enhanced safety for buyers.

Bangladesh President Mohammed Shahabuddin resigns, Hasina loses key ally in officeBangladesh President Mohammed Shahabuddin resigned on Friday, citing serious health issues, including autonomic neuropathy that causes occasional loss of consciousness.

US spy satellites made India abort nuclear test. Then, Buddha smiled againTwo decades after the Pokhran-I nuclear test in 1974, India was ready to test again in 1995. But American spy satellites spotted unusual activity at Pokhran before it could. This is why PM PV Narasimha Rao withheld the final clearance, and the move helped pave the way for the successful Pokhran-II tests three years later.

Dharmendra Pradhan won't quit, we'll deliver on people's mandate: Govt sourcesThere is "no question" of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigning over the NEET-UG paper leak row, government sources said, asserting that stepping down would be the easiest option and amount to running away from responsibility.

Usha Vance drops new family photos, introduces newborn son Alec Neel VanceJD Vance and Usha Vance have shared the first family photographs of their newborn son, Alec Neel Vance. The birth is notable as the first known case of a US second lady giving birth in office since 1870.

Be active on Instagram, post reels to connect with youth, PM tells ministers: SourcesPrime Minister Narendra Modi asked Union ministers to strengthen their Instagram presence and post reels to engage with young audiences more effectively, sources said, stressing that the platform is an important medium for public outreach.

The protest that brought soft-spoken Dimple Yadav into the spotlightSamajwadi Party MP Dimple Yadav's active involvement in the student protests has shown a different side of her. Known to keep a low profile, Dimple's unusually assertive role marked a visible political shift.

Live: 18 Delhi Metro stations to remain shut from 7:30 am today amid CJP stirCockroach Janta Party Nationwide Protest Live Updates: After a day of high political drama, the CJP-led agitation over the alleged NEET paper leak remains on edge as the Centre is set to announce major NTA reforms at 8 pm while another round of talks with protesters is scheduled for Saturday. CJP leaders claimed the government was positive on demands related to compensation and withdrawal of FIRs but sought more time to decide on Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. Meanwhile, Rahul Gandhi demanded the Prime Minister apologise over alleged pellet gun injuries to students, several opposition leaders rallied behind the protests, and the CJP warned that any attempt to detain its leaders ahead of the weekend would only intensify the movement. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates on the CJP protest.

What changed at Jantar Mantar after July 20? I visited the site 6 timesMy six visits to Jantar Mantar changed my perspective on what a protest can become. I marked July 1, July 10, July 15, July 19, July 21 and July 23 to visit the site and witness its evolution firsthand. In recent memory, few demonstrations have reached this scale. What began as an ordinary protest on Day 1 looked completely different by July 23, after the chaos of July 20. Young people from all walks of life arrived in solidarity, looking after one another and taking ownership of the movement.

Rohit Sharma is not retiring: BCCI vice-president Rajeev Shukla rubbishes rumoursRajeev Shukla said Rohit Sharma remains part of the Indian team and is continuing to play. The remarks came after Rohit's Lord's hundred and amid wider debate over India's leadership.

Indian sailor killed after ship attacked in Black Sea; govt condemns strikeThe Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) said the vessel, MV Omorfi, was sailing in Russian territorial waters when it was attacked, killing an Indian sailor. There were 10 crew members on board, including three Indian nationals.

Bengaluru CEO rides scooter inside office to celebrate company's 'amazing quarter'A Bengaluru-based CEO rode a scooty through the NoBroker office while celebrating his Packers and Movers team's record quarter, before handing it to the winning employee in a now-viral video.

sports.headline: Bowling like MS Dhoni: Ice-cool Putul Sonowal makes it 2 in 2 in lawn bowls Putul Sonowal won back-to-back tie-breakers on his Commonwealth Games debut in Glasgow. The Assam Police havildar's late-shot precision lifted India atop Section D and strengthened hopes of a medal run.

Rohit Sharma is not retiring: BCCI vice-president Rajeev Shukla rubbishes rumours Rajeev Shukla said Rohit Sharma remains part of the Indian team and is continuing to play. The remarks came after Rohit's Lord's hundred and amid wider debate over India's leadership.

Plot twist: Ashutosh Sharma turns unexpected bowling hero in England Ashutosh Sharma's stint in England has taken an unexpected turn, with the Hampshire recruit struggling with the bat but emerging as one of the One-Day Cup's standout bowlers.

Coach VVS Laxman introduces Best Attitude award in Zimbabwe. Here's 1st winner VVS Laxman introduced a Best Attitude Award during India's Zimbabwe T20I tour, with Tilak Varma winning first. India won the series opener in Harare on Thursday, beating Zimbabwe by seven wickets in Harare.

LeBron James heads to Philadelphia 76ers, says title pursuit drove final career move LeBron James will join the Philadelphia 76ers for his record-extending 24th NBA season, saying his final career move was driven by the pursuit of another championship rather than money or family.

Intolerable and unacceptable: Spain coach blasts Argentina for World Cup final brawl Spain coach Luis de la Fuente called Argentina's conduct "intolerable and unacceptable" after the World Cup final brawl, as FIFA launched an investigation into the post-match altercation following Spain's 1-0 victory.

India receive favourable routes to defend twin cricket golds at Asian Games India have received favourable draws in both the men's and women's cricket tournaments at the Aichi-Nagoya 2026 Asian Games as they aim to defend their twin gold medals. Both teams have avoided early heavyweight clashes, with potential India-Pakistan meetings possible only in the latter stages.

Durand Cup 2026: All you need to know as Kolkata Derby kicks off new season The 135th Durand Cup begins with the Kolkata Derby between East Bengal and Mohun Bagan Super Giant. Here's everything you need to know about the teams, format, groups, schedule and where to watch the tournament live.

Fourth-place curse strikes as Ashok Malik misses powerlifting medal Ashok Malik missed a Commonwealth Games para powerlifting medal despite a personal best 200kg lift in Glasgow on Friday. His fourth-place finish revived a familiar Indian sporting anguish.

Mayank Yadav, Ashok Sharma good additions to India's fast-bowling pool: Sunil Joshi Stand-in bowling coach Sunil Joshi hailed Mayank Yadav and Ashok Sharma as key additions to India's fast-bowling pool ahead of the second T20I against Zimbabwe, highlighting the importance of building pace depth amid workload management.

Klopp warns media he will quit Germany job over family intrusion Jurgen Klopp warned he will leave the Germany job if the media targets his family. The new head coach said family privacy is non-negotiable as he begins rebuilding the national side.

Srihari reaches 50m backstroke semifinals, para-swimmers Budinga, Ali in finalSrihari Nataraj scraped into the men's 50m backstroke semi-finals, while R V V B K Budigina and Imam Ali reached the S13 100m freestyle final. Budigina advanced after finishing third in the heats, and Ali also progressed as all eight swimmers went through.

Joe Root set to return as England Test captain after Ben Stokes retirementJoe Root is set to return as England's Test captain following Ben Stokes' retirement, with the ECB expected to appoint him for the Pakistan series starting on August 19 and likely through the 2027 Ashes.

India's Tulika Maan withdrawn from Glasgow CWG after NADA suspensionTulika Maan has been withdrawn from India's Glasgow Commonwealth Games judo squad after a provisional NADA suspension over three whereabouts failures. Her exit adds to India's disruption after Arun Kumar's suspension and dents the team's medal hopes.

Jurgen Klopp named Germany coach on deal until 2030 FIFA World CupGermany have turned to Jurgen Klopp to revive the national team after another disappointing World Cup campaign. The former Liverpool manager returns to the dugout on a deal that runs through the 2030 FIFA World Cup.

CWG Day 2 Live: Jhandu Kumar in strong contention for India's first medalCommonwealth Games 2026, Day 2 Live updates: Jhandu Kumar narrowly missed a Commonwealth Games record after his 196kg attempt was ruled a no lift for brushing the rack. The Indian still finished Group B with 130.9 points, a score that leaves him firmly in contention for the men's 72kg para powerlifting gold medal.

Mayank Yadav in awe of Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's 'unbelievable' battingMayank Yadav believes 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has the talent to reach the very top after watching him hammer his maiden international fifty. The India pacer called the teenager's fearless stroke-play "unbelievable."

Viswanathan Anand becomes interim FIDE president after EU sanctions DvorkovichViswanathan Anand has taken over as interim FIDE president after EU sanctions hit Arkady Dvorkovich. Anand was working as the deputy FIDE chief after Dvorkovich came into power.

Formula 1 return to India nears as FMSCI gets key government nodIndia's Formula 1 ambitions received a major boost after the Sports Ministry granted provisional NSF recognition to FMSCI. The move strengthens the governing body's role as the government targets an F1 return by 2028.

Italy tried to appoint Ancelotti for manager before approaching GuardiolaItaly's coaching search began with an ambitious move for Carlo Ancelotti before the FIGC turned to Pep Guardiola after Brazil ruled out the veteran's departure. Paolo Maldini says the federation wanted to start with "the best in the world."

No all-out push at CWG 2026: Mirabai Chanu prioritises Asian GamesMirabai Chanu said she will not chase a 90kg snatch at the Commonwealth Games, keeping her build-up intact for the Asian Games. Chanu is aiming to secure her third consecutive gold medal at the CWG.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's mystery celebration decoded after historic fiftyVaibhav Sooryavanshi has explained the reason behind his mystery celebration after his historic knock during the first T20I against Zimbabwe. Sooryavanshi went past Sachin Tendulkar with his blitz that helped India win the match.

Could the Ashes be played in India? Cricket Australia open to lucrative ideaTodd Greenberg said Cricket Australia would consider staging an Australia vs England Test in India. The financial appeal of the Indian market has been on the rise in recent times with the Big Bash League staging a match in Chennai later this year.

Commonwealth Games Lite: Why 10 sports were axed from Glasgow 2026As the 2026 Commonwealth Games get underway in Glasgow, a drastically reduced sports list tests a new, low-cost formula to save the event—while taking away India's biggest medal-winning events.

From World No.1 to penniless: How tennis icon Arantxa Vicario became bankruptArantxa Sanchez Vicario has blamed former husband Josep Santacana for the loss of her fortune and the debt case that followed. The former world No. 1 says she is still repaying the bank, a fall that turned a glittering career into a fight for survival.

India at Commonwealth Games: July 24 schedule, medal events, fixtures in GlasgowIndia's campaign at the Commonwealth Games 2026 gathers momentum on Friday, July 24, with medal opportunities in para powerlifting and a packed schedule across gymnastics, swimming, lawn bowls and boxing in Glasgow.

Commonwealth Games declared open as Mirabai, Lovlina lead India at Parade of NationsOlympic medallists Mirabai Chanu and Lovlina Borgohain led India into the Commonwealth Games 2026 opening ceremony to a thunderous reception as King Charles III officially declared the Games open at the OVO Hydro in Glasgow on Wednesday.

Childhood dream kept me motivated: Mayank Yadav 2.0 impresses on injury comebackIndia pacer Mayank Yadav has opened up on the mental toll of his two-year injury layoff, describing the prolonged absence from international cricket as the toughest phase of his young career after making an impressive return against Zimbabwe in the first T20I on Thursday.

Highlights: Lovlina, Mirabai lead India's contingent in styleCommonwealth Games 2026 Highlights: The Commonwealth Games 2026 began with a star-studded opening ceremony at Glasgow's OVO Hydro Arena. Around 3,000 athletes from 74 nations, including a strong Indian contingent led by Mirabai Chanu, will mark the start of the 11-day spectacle.

Our generation deserves every opportunity to learn: Shubman Gill on NEET protestsShubman Gill shared a message calling for compassion, mutual respect and greater opportunities for young people as student protests continue, while stressing the importance of education and peaceful expression.

PV Sindhu calls out China Open scheduling after Chen Yufei ends her campaignPV Sindhu questioned the China Open scheduling after her second-round exit on Thursday, highlighting the disparity in recovery time between players with a tongue-in-cheek post on social media following her defeat to home favourite Chen Yufei.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
